name: dual-canvas-pointer-toggle
description: Two stacked rendering surfaces: MapLibre GL canvas underneath, Excalidraw canvas on top. pointerEvents CSS toggled to route input to correct surface. maxPitch:0 required at MapLibre construction — map.project() IS perspective-correct under pitch, so fix is construction option not runtime workaround.

name: annotation-vs-data-layer
description: Annotation layer = Excalidraw elements (ephemeral, screen-anchored, drawn). Data layer = GeoJSON FeatureCollections managed by YjsLayer (persistent, geo-anchored, imported). GeoJSON FeatureCollection is a snapshot projection of the Yjs doc, not source of truth. This dichotomy is load-bearing across all phases — never conflate the two stores.

name: worker-prelude-sandbox
description: Web Worker plugins: null fetch, XHR, WebSocket, and importScripts in prelude before plugin entry point executes. Worker sandbox is weaker than spec assumed — these APIs are retained unless explicitly overridden. Required for Phase 7 plugin isolation.

title: Single-player mode is first-class deployment
rationale: WebSocket opt-in via [realtime] enabled=true in config.toml. apps/atlas-app must function with no VITE_WS_URL. Collab UI degrades gracefully. Phase 4 ships single-player Docker only; realtime added Phase 5. Supports laptop/airplane-mode story and trivial demo: docker run atlasdraw/atlas-app needs no postgres/minio/realtime.

title: Hosted flagship by v1.0, no open-core split
rationale: Plausible-model: all features in OSS under AGPL. Hosted funds maintainer time and provides empirical perf baseline. Billing/workspace in Phase 6 but off-by-default in self-host. Telemetry must be opt-out for self-hosters, wholly omitted from embed SDK. Rules out n8n-model exclusive hosted features.

title: Phase 1 extended to 4 weeks; all subsequent phases shifted +1
rationale: Event routing across stacked canvases (MapLibre + Excalidraw pointerEvents) is a high-churn Excalidraw subsystem. Week 5 carved for event-routing hardening with E2E browser matrix gate. Phases 2-6 shifted +1 from spec baseline. Phase 7 uses months 7-12 (weeks 26+).

name: Safari URL hash limit for share-via-URL
description: Safari URL hash limit ~50,000 chars. lz-string compresses 32KB AtlasdrawDocument to ~17.5K chars (2.8x margin). Share-via-URL feasible for typical maps; large maps need server-side share token.

Demo install regressed monorepo typecheck — apps/atlas-app skeleton install upgraded TypeScript via vite peer-deps; yarn test:typecheck now fails monorepo-wide on TS5107 (moduleResolution=node10 deprecated). Expected: adding a demo app into an existing monorepo slot is safe. Got: peer-dep resolution upgraded root TypeScript version, breaking all packages.
resolution: unresolved — three candidates: ignoreDeprecations:5.0 in tsconfig.base.json (fastest, punts deprecation); pin TS in root yarn.lock (blocks future vite bumps); migrate to moduleResolution:bundler (correct, riskier). Tracked: atlasdraw-ca94

title: firebase-project/ KEEP through Phase 2 boundary
rationale: 4 files, 34 lines total, zero monorepo references, pristine upstream Excalidraw firestore deployment config. Sonnet verification recommended KEEP; Opus agreed. Re-evaluate explicitly at Phase 2 boundary when excalidraw-app/ is replaced — not before.

title: keep local — no GitHub push or commit until atlasdraw-6e33 resolved
rationale: User explicit instruction: no gh repo create, no git push, no commit this session. Phase 0 + Wave 1 stays uncommitted in working tree. Deferred indefinitely pending atlasdraw-6e33 (GitHub org decision).

title: demo wired into apps/atlas-app skeleton, not a standalone testbed
rationale: apps/atlas-app already had package.json + tsconfig; cheaper to extend than scaffold a new testbed. The demo TS regression is the accepted cost of this choice — it would have been isolated in a standalone testbed but wall-clock time was faster here.

description: Excalidraw v0.18 viewBackgroundColor is an AppState field, not a top-level prop — expected: <Excalidraw viewBackgroundColor='transparent'/> silently accepted (TypeScript permits unknown props on loose ExcalidrawProps); got: prop silently ignored, canvas painted opaque white (#ffffff default). AppState fields (viewBackgroundColor, gridSize, theme, scrollModeEnabled) MUST go via initialData.appState, not as direct JSX props. ExcalidrawProps type at types.ts:570 is loose enough to accept unknown props without compile error.
resolution: Pass via initialData.appState: <Excalidraw initialData={{ appState: { viewBackgroundColor: 'transparent' } }} />. Rule: before using any Excalidraw JSX prop, grep ExcalidrawProps interface at code/packages/excalidraw/types.ts:570 to confirm it exists there — if not found, it is an AppState field and belongs in initialData.appState.

content: New app workspace consuming @excalidraw/excalidraw source requires three setup steps: (1) declare @excalidraw/excalidraw as a workspace dep in package.json, (2) add 5-package alias map to vite.config.ts mirroring code/excalidraw-app/vite.config.mts — aliases for @excalidraw/{common,element,excalidraw,math,utils} pointing to ../../packages/{common,element,excalidraw,math,utils}/index.tsx (paths relative to app depth), (3) add sass as devDep so .scss imports inside packages/excalidraw/index.tsx resolve. Without all three: build fails or canvas paints opaque. Reference: code/apps/atlas-app/vite.config.ts.

name: excalidraw-imperativeapi-onchange-pattern
description: ExcalidrawImperativeAPI.onChange(callback) returns an UnsubscribeCallback (types.ts:971). Use this for Excalidraw state subscriptions from hooks instead of prop-drilling onChange through the <Excalidraw> component. Pattern: call api.onChange(cb) in useEffect, store the returned unsubscribe fn, call it in cleanup. Seed initial state synchronously from api.getAppState() before the first onChange fires. Default activeTool.type is 'selection' per appState.ts getDefaultAppState — isDrawingMode=false on first render, map pannable immediately. Reference: code/apps/atlas-app/src/hooks/useToolState.ts.

name: excalidraw-initialdata-module-scope-const
description: Hoist Excalidraw initialData object to module scope as a const (EXCALIDRAW_INITIAL_DATA) rather than defining it inline in the component body or as a useMemo. Rationale: if Excalidraw ever memoizes on initialData reference, an inline object literal creates a new reference on every render, potentially triggering canvas reset. Module-scope const is stable across all renders at zero cost. Reference: code/apps/atlas-app/src/components/MapEditor.tsx.

title: atlasdraw tools dispatch independently of Excalidraw tool system via overlay
rationale: Excalidraw v0.18 has no customTools prop (confirmed by vendored source grep). Atlas-side tools (PinTool, future PolygonTool/LineTool) cannot register into Excalidraw's tool lifecycle. Integration model: (1) useAtlasdrawTool hook owns activeAtlasTool state + builds ToolContext façade; (2) .atlasToolOverlay div sits z-index above Excalidraw, captures pointerdown when atlas tool active; (3) seedToElement bridges AtlasdrawElementSeed→ExcalidrawElement then calls excalidrawAPI.updateScene. PinTool itself is pure — no React/DOM/maplibre dependency. This pattern applies to all future atlas tools. Excalidraw's own tool system (selection, hand, rectangle, etc.) is untouched.

description: Excalidraw v0.18 customTools prop does not exist — expected: <Excalidraw customTools={...}/> accepted by TypeScript and registered tool in Excalidraw toolbar; got: ExcalidrawProps has no customTools field; prop silently ignored; no runtime error; tool integration non-functional. Third plan-literal-divergence instance (after viewBackgroundColor Wave 3a, newElementWith Wave 3b). TypeScript does not catch it because ExcalidrawProps is loose.
resolution: Atlas-side dispatch via overlay (mx-682f8a): useAtlasdrawTool + .atlasToolOverlay div + seedToElement bridge. PinTool ships without touching Excalidraw tool system. Convention mx-145cd3 codifies the grep-first rule that caught this before Worker 14 dispatch.

name: appState-newElement-draft-guard
description: appState.newElement is the Excalidraw field that signals an element is being actively drafted (between pointerDown and pointerUp). When non-null, the bbox is incomplete (first-frame click, not final drag extent). useGeoAnchor uses this to skip geo-stamping until pointerUp completes the bbox: if (appState.newElement) return early. Without this guard, geo coords are stamped on the first click pixel, not the final rectangle extent. Field is null between elements. Reference: code/apps/atlas-app/src/hooks/useGeoAnchor.ts.

title: Zustand+immer for cross-component reactive state; module-singleton for tool-internal state
rationale: Wave 2 LayerRegistry decision (user-confirmed Option A): Zustand+immer for LayerRegistry because it is cross-component shared reactive state — multiple readers (LayerPanel, MapEditor drop handler, Convert), multiple mutators, mutation-heavy (visibility toggles, reorder, style edits). This is a DIFFERENT problem class from Wave 1b's module-singleton pattern (mx-d06b22), which applies to per-tool internal state with single-pointer/single-active-tool invariant. The two patterns coexist: module-singleton remains correct for tool-internal state; Zustand+immer is the choice when 3+ components must react to the same mutation. Adding Zustand+immer is a one-dep expansion (~3KB); reinventing it with useSyncExternalStore would duplicate its wheel for the cross-component case.

content: Data layer IDs use dl:${crypto.randomUUID()} format; mint at call site, NOT inside registry — T11, T13, and T14 all mint data layer IDs at the call site using 'dl:' + crypto.randomUUID(). The 'dl:' prefix marks MapLibre source/layer IDs owned by atlasdraw (distinguishable from Excalidraw element IDs and other map sources). ID generation is the caller's responsibility; LayerRegistry.registerDataLayer accepts a pre-minted id as a parameter rather than generating it internally. Rationale: caller-controlled ID lets T14 (convertAnnotationToDataLayer) preserve a predictable relationship between the source annotation element ID and the derived data layer ID; registry-generated IDs would break this traceability.

name: customData-data-escape-hatch-seedToElement
description: customData._data escape-hatch in seedToElement.ts — T14 plan literal said radiusKm lives at customData.radiusKm; actual location is customData._data.radiusKm, an escape-hatch nested field set by the PinTool seed-to-element bridge at seedToElement.ts:131. T14 was updated to read both shapes (customData.radiusKm ?? customData._data?.radiusKm) for forward compatibility. The _data sub-object is the canonical storage for tool-specific payload fields in the current bridge implementation; plan authors should check seedToElement.ts:131 before assuming flat customData key paths.

name: capture-phase native listener for deeper-DOM third-party event interception
description: When a third-party component (e.g. Excalidraw) renders a div deeper in the DOM than your root container, its bubble-phase handlers fire BEFORE any React onDrop/onDragOver on the ancestor — innermost element bubbles first. Excalidraw's handleAppOnDrop (App.tsx:2147, 11872) consumes dataTransfer.files before the ancestor bubble handler sees the event. Fix: replace React event props with native addEventListener('drop'/'dragover', handler, { capture: true }) on the root div. Capture fires top-down; your handler runs before any bubble-phase target. For file types you own: preventDefault + stopPropagation so the third party never sees the event. For other types: allow propagation. dragover capture calls preventDefault unconditionally (required for drop to fire) but NOT stopPropagation (preserves third-party visual cues). File: code/apps/atlas-app/src/components/MapEditor.tsx.

name: multi-system mutation atomicity: pure-compute → may-throw+rollback → in-memory → destructive-last
description: When a single operation mutates multiple systems (map source/layer, in-memory registry, external scene API), order steps by reversibility: Step 1 — pure compute only (annotationToFeatureCollection, id generation, style derivation); Step 2 — most-likely-to-throw with explicit rollback (map.addSource, then map.addLayer — if addLayer throws, removeSource to undo Step 2); Step 3 — in-memory registry mutations (registerDataLayer + remove — won't throw, safe after map succeeds); Step 4 — destructive external API call last (excalidrawAPI.updateScene — removes original annotation from canvas). Rationale: if any step throws, prior steps are already rolled back or haven't happened yet, preventing orphaned registry state or deleted-but-not-replaced canvas elements. Pattern generalizes beyond MapEditor: any time a sequence includes a may-throw, a registry, and a side-effectful external call, this ordering prevents partial-success corruption. File: code/apps/atlas-app/src/components/MapEditor.tsx (handleConvert).

name: Excalidraw handleAppOnDrop location — App.tsx:2147,11872
description: Excalidraw v0.18 drop handling lives in two places in code/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx: the handler definition near line 2147 and its registration near line 11872 on a div deeper than MapEditor's root container. This div being deeper means its bubble-phase onDrop fires before any ancestor React handler. dataTransfer.files is consumed by parseDataTransferEvent inside handleAppOnDrop, leaving the ancestor with an empty FileList. Cross-reference: capture-phase pattern (mx-8ec4d1) is the structural fix.

content: No single state/store.ts in atlas-app — pattern is per-concern Zustand stores. state/layerRegistry.ts exports useLayerRegistryStore; T9 added state/usePersistenceStore.ts as a peer. Each concern gets its own create<...>()(immer((set) => ({...}))). Plan references to 'modify state/store.ts' are always wrong — the file does not exist.

App.tsx is a one-liner <MapEditor /> — the actual change-site for any Excalidraw composition is code/apps/atlas-app/src/components/MapEditor.tsx (~line 470+) where <Excalidraw> is mounted. Plan references to 'modify App.tsx (MainMenu composition)' are always wrong. App.tsx has no direct Excalidraw wiring.

Data-layer FeatureCollections live in MapLibre sources at runtime, not in any registry. useLayerRegistryStore stores only metadata + featureCount, not the FC itself. T9 selectDocument() ships layers:new Map() for v1 because there is no FC store to read from. Phase 4 needs a parallel FC registry keyed by dl: id to bridge document persistence and map rendering.

AtlasdrawDocument.scene typed via structural alias SceneElement = { id: string; type: string; version: number; [key:string]: unknown } in @atlasdraw/data/manifest-schema.ts. Excalidraw's OrderedExcalidrawElement is structurally assignable to it; consumer-side updateScene typechecks without circular dep. Avoids polluting CLI deps with vendored Excalidraw types. Pattern: when @atlasdraw/data needs to typify Excalidraw output without importing it, declare a structural minimum-fields alias.

Data-layer FeatureCollection mirroring lives in LayerRegistry actions (registerDataLayer, convertAnnotationToDataLayer, remove), NOT in useLayerRegistrySync hook. The hook never sees FC payloads — MapEditor.tsx does. Registry actions already receive FC as a parameter — cleanest seam. Pattern: when wiring a derived store, trace the data-flow path and insert at the point of authority, not the point of consumption.

Excalidraw v0.18 vendored Scene.replaceAllElements ALWAYS validates fractional indices via validateFractionalIndices and throws InvalidFractionalIndexError if any element neighbor's index field is undefined. ANY caller that constructs new ExcalidrawElements (Pin tool, future custom tools, hydrate path, etc.) MUST wrap the elements array with syncInvalidIndices from @excalidraw/element BEFORE calling excalidrawAPI.updateScene. Mirrors restore.ts:704, reconcile.ts, scene/export.ts:279 — Excalidraw's own internal pattern. Discovered by playwright-cli smoke test 2026-05-06 (commit ae1a15c). Tests using mocked updateScene WILL NOT catch this — only real-Excalidraw integration test surfaces the validator.
